title: What is a gene?: MedlinePlus Genetics
text:
names can be long, genes are also assigned symbols, which are short combinations of letters (and sometimes numbers) that represent an abbreviated version of the gene name. For example, one of the genes on chromosome 7 is called the cystic fibrosis transmembrane conductance regulator because variants in this gene can cause cystic fibrosis . The symbol for this gene is Â  CFTR .
Genes are made up of DNA. Each chromosome contains many genes.
Credit: U.S. National Library of Medicine 
For more information about genes: 
MedlinePlus Genetics provides consumer-friendly gene summaries that include an explanation of each gene's normal function and how variants in the gene cause particular genetic conditions. 
More information about how genetic conditions and genes are named is also available from MedlinePlus Genetics. 
The National Human Genome Research Institute (NHGRI) provides information about the Human Genome Project and Telomere 2 Telomere , two studies that were pivotal in providing a complete sequence of the human genome. NHGRI also includes general information about genes and the genome . 
The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) offers basic information aboutÂ genetics .
An article published in the journal Science in 2022, â The complete sequence of a human genome ,âÂ provides updated information on the human genome.
